texto: Somebodys said that it couldn't be done But he with a chuckle replied That "maybe it couldn't " but he would be one Who wouldn't say so till he'd tried. So he buckled right in with the trace of a grin On his face. If he worried he hid it. He started to sing as he tackled the thing That couldn't be done and he did it.

So he buckled right in with the trace of a grin On his face. If he worried he hid it. He started to sing as he tackled the thing That couldn't be done and he did it.

texto: At ChristmasA man is at his finest towards the finish of the year;He is almost what he should be when the Christmas season's here;Then he's thinking more of others than he's thought the months before,And the laughter of his children is a joy worth toiling for.He is less a selfish creature than at any other time;When the Christmas spirit rules him he comes close to the sublime.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Edgar A. Guest sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “At ChristmasA man is at his finest towards the finish of the year;He is almost what he should be …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, christmas, christmasa, útil em momentos de natal e reflexao.

texto: I have to live with myself and so, I want to be fit for myself to know.
